evals.name: init-happy-path
evals.prompt: Set up Kinoa for this project. The game ID is aaaaaaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaaaaaaaaaa, the game secret is FAKE_GAME_SECRET, the bearer token is FAKE_BEARER_TOKEN.
evals.expected_output: Integration type is hardcoded to API; the skill MUST NOT ask 'API or SDK' via AskUserQuestion. kinoa_init.py is invoked WITHOUT --integration-type (the flag was removed) and with --game-id aaaaaaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaaaaaaaaaa --game-secret FAKE_GAME_SECRET --bearer-token FAKE_BEARER_TOKEN. ~/.kinoa/session.env is written with KINOA_INTEGRATION_TYPE=API, KINOA_GAME_ID, KINOA_GAME_SECRET, KINOA_BEARER_TOKEN (mode 0600). Validation calls GET dashboard.kinoa.io/gamemetaapi/api/game-settings with three headers: Authorization: Bearer <token>, Game: <uuid>, AND Game-Id: <uuid> — both Game and Game-Id MUST be present and carry the same UUID. With a fake bearer, validation returns 401 with reason='unauthorized'; the skill surfaces this cleanly and asks the user to recheck the bearer.

evals.name: init-fix-integration-type-to-api
evals.prompt: Run init. Game id aaaaaaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaaaaaaaaaa, game secret FAKE_GAME_SECRET, bearer FAKE_BEARER_TOKEN. Assume the project's actual integration_type on the server is currently SDK (a leftover from before the SDK option was removed).
evals.expected_output: Skill does NOT ask integration type. kinoa_init.py runs without --integration-type, expecting 'API'. First validation returns http_status=200 with integration_type='SDK' on the server, so reason='wrong_integration_type' (expected_integration_type='API'). The skill asks via AskUserQuestion whether to switch the project to API now (it should NOT offer SDK as an option). On yes, it re-runs with --fix-integration-type which POSTs {"integrationType":"API"} to game-settings, then re-validates and reports ok=true. All HTTP calls send the dual Game + Game-Id headers in addition to Authorization: Bearer.

evals.name: sync-player-fields-list-and-diff
evals.prompt: Run the player-fields sync against my project. Pretend init has already been done and my Player class has fields: player_id, level, profile.country_code, wallet.gold.
evals.expected_output: The kinoa-sync-player-fields-integration workflow calls the sibling kinoa-dashboard-player-fields helper (../kinoa-dashboard-player-fields/kinoa_dashboard_player_fields.py) with subcommands list-predefined and list-custom. For predefined fields with state=not_implemented whose path matches the app (e.g., 'level', 'personal_info.country_code'), it proposes 🟢 ACTIVATE or 🟡 IMPLEMENT+ACTIVATE. For predefined fields with state=active not in the app, it surfaces 🟠 WARNING. For app fields with no matching predefined path (e.g., 'wallet.gold'), it proposes 🔵 CREATE CUSTOM. The developer is presented a numbered checklist before any state-changing call is made (those calls go through kinoa-dashboard-player-fields too). After C.4 applies, Phase C.5 invokes ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/generate_report.py with a JSON payload containing four buckets — predefined_integrated, predefined_not_integrated, custom_integrated, custom_not_integrated — and writes a timestamped HTML file to the project cwd (./kinoa-player-fields-integration-report-*.html). The skill surfaces the absolute path to the developer.

evals.name: open-session-fresh
evals.prompt: Open a Kinoa session for player Illcko at level 1, with custom_field=custom_value. (Pretend init has already been run with valid credentials.)
evals.expected_output: kinoa_open_session.py is invoked with --player-id Illcko --level 1 --field custom_field=custom_value. The script generates a UUID for session_id, POSTs to https://gate.kinoa.io/playerevents/api/v3/player/session/start with headers {game: <secret>, session_id: <uuid>} and body {player_state: {player_identifiers: {player_id: 'Illcko'}, level: 1, custom_field: 'custom_value'}}. After a successful response, KINOA_LAST_PLAYER_ID and KINOA_LAST_SESSION_ID are persisted to ~/.kinoa/session.env. Server-side this also auto-fires session_start. This step in the integration flow is the prerequisite for the next eval (sync-events) — once the application has wired open-session into its own runtime code, sync-events can detect that and auto-publish session_start.

evals.name: sync-events-list-and-diff
evals.prompt: Run the events sync against my project. Pretend init has already been done with KINOA_INTEGRATION_TYPE=API. My app code emits these events: level_up, install, tutorial, gold_purchase. The app also implements open-session (POSTs directly to /playerevents/api/v3/player/session/start).
evals.expected_output: The kinoa-sync-event-integration workflow calls the sibling kinoa-dashboard-event helper (../kinoa-dashboard-event/kinoa_dashboard_event.py) with subcommands list-predefined and list-custom. Phase A reads KINOA_INTEGRATION_TYPE=API from ~/.kinoa/session.env, greps the source for the direct endpoint URL fragment (playerevents/api/v3/player/session/start), finds it, and sets SESSION_START_AUTO_FIRES=True. The diff applies two pre-rules: (1) session_start is treated as in-app because SESSION_START_AUTO_FIRES is True (server auto-fires it on the open-session POST), (2) watch_ad/install/payment are tagged ⭐ HIGHLY RECOMMENDED with a callout explaining they unlock ad-revenue / install attribution / monetization analytics. Diff buckets fire: ⭐🟢 install (NOT_IMPLEMENTED, app emits), ⭐🟡 payment (NOT_IMPLEMENTED, not in app — needs implementing), ⭐🟠 watch_ad (ACTIVE in Kinoa, app doesn't emit), 🟢 tutorial (publish), 🔄 session_start (auto-publish — no KinoaEvents entry, no emission site, the server fires it), 🟣 test event (active custom in Kinoa not in app), 🔵 gold_purchase (app-only, create custom), and 🟡 for other predefined NOT_IMPLEMENTED events. ⭐ rows are pulled to top of the C.3 checklist. After C.4 applies, C.5 asks via AskUserQuestion whether to use full or diff player_state strategy. C.6 invokes ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/generate_report.py with a JSON payload containing critical_events (session_start/payment/watch_ad/install with integrated true|false) plus the four buckets (predefined_integrated, predefined_not_integrated, custom_integrated, custom_not_integrated). The script writes a timestamped HTML file to the project cwd (./kinoa-event-integration-report-*.html). The skill surfaces the absolute path. Phase D test scenarios use the local kinoa_send_event.py (same folder) to fire a verification event.

evals.name: sync-events-no-direct-endpoint
evals.prompt: Run the events sync. Init was already done. The app opens sessions via an internal helper class SessionOpener.openForPlayer(...) — there are NO calls in the source to gate.kinoa.io/playerevents/api/v3/player/session/start. App emits: level_up, gold_purchase.
evals.expected_output: Phase A only branches on whether the app uses the direct /player/session/start endpoint (the API/SDK distinction has been removed — integration type is always API). Greps the source for the URL fragment 'playerevents/api/v3/player/session/start' or 'player/session/start' — finds nothing. Sets SESSION_START_AUTO_FIRES=False. Phase C.2 classifies session_start normally — since the app does not yet emit it as a regular event, it falls into 🟡 implement+publish. Phase C.4 uses the 🔁 explicit-emit branch: add session_start to KinoaEvents, wire an emission site immediately AFTER SessionOpener.openForPlayer(...) returns successfully (not before — emitting before a session is open is a usage error), then publish via kinoa-dashboard-event publish. The 🔄 auto-publish action is NOT used in this case.

evals.name: sync-event-integration-phase-d-test-send
evals.prompt: I'm in Phase D of the events integration — verifying my level_up event lands. Fire a level_up using the workflow's runtime test helper. The level params (level=10, place=arena) come from level_up's predefined schema and the operator-added param promo_code=SUMMER is operator-defined. Use the diff player_state strategy: send only the changed field, level=10.
evals.expected_output: The skill (kinoa-sync-event-integration Phase D) invokes its local runtime helper kinoa_send_event.py (located in the same folder, ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/kinoa_send_event.py) with --name level_up --system-param level=10 --system-param place=arena --param promo_code=SUMMER --player-state level=10. The body POSTed to https://gate.kinoa.io/playerevents/api/v3/sync-event?player_id=<KINOA_LAST_PLAYER_ID> has shape: {event: {event_data: {name: 'level_up', session_id: <KINOA_LAST_SESSION_ID>, level: '10', place: 'arena', custom_params: {promo_code: 'SUMMER'}}, player_state: {level: '10'}}}. System params (level, place) land at top of event_data; custom param (promo_code) lands nested under event_data.custom_params; player_state diff lands under event.player_state. After the send, the skill verifies receipt by fetching the event record via kinoa-dashboard-event get and the player state via kinoa-dashboard-player-fields get-player-state.

evals.name: player-fields-c5-report-generation
evals.prompt:
I'm at Phase C.5 of the player-fields sync — Phase C.4 just finished. Generate the HTML sync report. Pretend init is done and KINOA_GAME_ID is aaaaaaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaaaaaaaaaa.

Phase A discovered KinoaPlayerState at src/main/kotlin/KinoaPlayerState.kt with paths: player_id, level, personal_info.country_code, wallet.gold.

Phase C.1 list-predefined returned: [{name:'Level', path:'level', kind:'number', state:'not_implemented'}, {name:'Country code', path:'personal_info.country_code', kind:'enumeration', state:'not_implemented', extra:'US,UK,DE,JP'}, {name:'Last event time', path:'last_event_time', kind:'date', state:'active'}, {name:'Total spend', path:'total_spend', kind:'number', state:'not_implemented'}].

Phase C.1 list-custom returned: [{name:'vip_tier', path:'vip_tier', kind:'number', state:'active'}, {name:'abandoned_field', path:'abandoned_field', kind:'string', state:'active'}].

Phase C.4 actions taken: activated 'level' (newly activated), implemented+activated 'personal_info.country_code' (added to KinoaPlayerState then activated), created custom 'gold_balance' at path 'wallet.gold' (kind: number, newly created), developer skipped activating 'total_spend' and skipped mirroring 'abandoned_field'. 'last_event_time' is the 🟠 warning (active in Kinoa, not in code). 'vip_tier' was added to KinoaPlayerState in C.4 — wait, no, the developer skipped it too.

Generate the C.5 report and surface the absolute path of the HTML file. Then offer to run the review loop.
evals.expected_output: The skill assembles a JSON payload with the four buckets and pipes it to ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/generate_report.py. predefined_integrated has 2 items (level newly-activated, personal_info.country_code implemented+activated). predefined_not_integrated has 2 items: total_spend (state=not_implemented, note='skipped by developer') and last_event_time (state=active, note about active-in-Kinoa-but-missing-in-code warning). custom_integrated has 1 item (gold_balance newly-created at wallet.gold). custom_not_integrated has 2 items (vip_tier and abandoned_field, both active in Kinoa, not mirrored in code). Output path matches ./kinoa-player-fields-integration-report-*.html in cwd, with timestamp suffix. After the script returns ok=true, the skill prints the absolute path and asks via AskUserQuestion (or its narrative equivalent) whether the developer wants to integrate more fields now.

evals.name: events-c6-report-generation
evals.prompt:
I'm at Phase C.6 of the events sync — C.4 and C.5 just finished. Generate the events sync HTML report. Pretend init is done, KINOA_GAME_ID is aaaaaaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaaaaaaaaaa, SESSION_START_AUTO_FIRES=True (app uses recommended endpoint), player_state strategy chosen in C.5 was DIFF, KinoaEvents is at src/main/kotlin/KinoaEvents.kt.

App emits these events: level_up, install, gold_purchase. Kinoa list-predefined returned (truncated for relevance): session_start [params: device_id:string(system), time:number(system)], level_up [params: level:number(system), place:string(system), tier:enumeration(system, extra='bronze,silver,gold')], install [params: source:string(system), campaign:string(system)], watch_ad [params: ad_type:enumeration(system, extra='rewarded,interstitial,banner'), ad_income:number(system)], payment [params: amount:number(system), currency:string(system)]. session_start, level_up, install statuses are NOT_IMPLEMENTED before C.4. watch_ad has status=ACTIVE (was integrated by another teammate previously). payment is NOT_IMPLEMENTED. Custom events from list-custom: vip_promotion [params: tier:string(custom), promo_id:string(custom)], status active.

C.4 actions: published session_start (auto-fire branch), published level_up, implemented+published install, created custom gold_purchase [params: amount:number(custom), pack:enumeration(custom, extra='small,medium,large')]. Developer skipped payment.

Generate the report and surface the absolute path. Then offer the review loop.
evals.expected_output: The skill assembles JSON and pipes to ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/generate_report.py. critical_events array contains all four — session_start integrated:true (auto-fired by server), install integrated:true (newly published), watch_ad integrated:true (already active in Kinoa, app emits indirectly — actually NO, the app does NOT emit watch_ad, so this should be NOT integrated; wait, watch_ad is ACTIVE in Kinoa from a previous teammate but the app code does not emit it → 🟠 WARNING bucket → integrated:false), payment integrated:false (skipped). So 2 of 4 critical events show as not-integrated; the report's critical section renders RED. predefined_integrated bucket: session_start, level_up, install. predefined_not_integrated: watch_ad (status=ACTIVE, note about warning), payment (status=NOT_IMPLEMENTED, note about skipped). custom_integrated: gold_purchase. custom_not_integrated: vip_promotion. Each row's params field is the FULL parameter list with system+custom combined: e.g., level_up has [{name:'level', kind:'number', system:true}, {name:'place', kind:'string', system:true}, {name:'tier', kind:'enumeration', system:true, extra:'bronze,silver,gold'}]. Output path matches ./kinoa-event-integration-report-*.html in cwd. After ok=true, the skill surfaces the absolute path and asks the developer (via AskUserQuestion or narrative equivalent) whether to integrate more events now.
